tldr: Move raw object storage to a media-aware platform: Cloudinary handles upload, on-the-fly transformation, CDN delivery, and AI tagging — best when you want managed media features rather than DIY processing on top of S3.
why_switch: You want on-the-fly image and video transformations without managing a pipeline
You need built-in CDN delivery tied to media assets
You want AI tagging and auto-format/quality features out of the box
You prefer fewer moving parts than S3 + Lambda + CloudFront
You want SDK-driven uploads instead of pre-signed URL plumbing
what_to_export: Bucket inventory (object keys, sizes, content types)
Bucket and object metadata you rely on
Existing public URL patterns (for redirect mapping)
ACL/permission model (to translate to Cloudinary access controls)
Any CloudFront/CDN config tied to S3
Tag taxonomy if you classify assets
export_method: Use the official AWS CLI (aws s3 sync or aws s3 cp --recursive) or AWS S3 Batch Operations to enumerate and pull originals to a staging location. For very large buckets, consult the official AWS docs on S3 Inventory and Batch Operations rather than driving everything client-side.
import_method: Upload to Cloudinary via the official upload API or SDKs (cloudinary npm, server SDKs in other languages). For bulk migration, Cloudinary documents an auto-upload/fetch URL feature that pulls remote sources on first request — check current Cloudinary docs for exact behavior, limits, and naming. Preserve your folder structure via the public_id field.

concept_mapping.note: Cloudinary uses a single 'cloud' per account with folders inside; consult official docs for current structure.
Set public_id on upload to preserve paths.
Cloudinary supports signed URLs — check docs for the current signing scheme.
Replace custom processing with Cloudinary transformation parameters in the URL.
Delivery is integrated; no separate CDN to manage.
Cloudinary doesn't expose tiered storage classes the same way — review pricing page.
Public/authenticated access is plan- and setting-dependent — consult docs.

effort: 1-3 weeks for a moderate bucket (hundreds of GB, app code updates included); larger or media-heavy migrations longer.
gotchas: Cloudinary URL transforms create lock-in — URLs become tightly coupled to the provider
Egress from S3 is billed — plan migration windows to control cost
Pricing model changes (storage + transformations + bandwidth) — model your usage on Cloudinary's official pricing page
Update all app code that built S3 URLs or used the AWS SDK
If you used S3 Versioning, replicate that policy with Cloudinary's versioning features — check current behavior
Existing public URLs need redirects until clients update

email.subject: Migrating media assets from S3 to Cloudinary
email.body:
Hi team,

We're moving image and video assets from S3 to Cloudinary so we can drop our custom transform pipeline. Plan: inventory the bucket, bulk-upload via Cloudinary's API (preserving paths through public_id), update SDK calls, and add redirects for the old URL patterns. Expect a 1-3 week window. I'll share a checklist before kickoff.

Thanks
